message.content: i previously had some i18n work but my computer crashed. where are we in that terms?

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt: Search for any i18n (internationalization) work in the checkpoint history and transcripts. Look for mentions of i18n, internationalization, translations, language support, react-i18next, i18next, or any related setup. Report what was done, what files were changed, and what the current state was. Be thorough.

content.text:
The Entire CLI is installed but requires authentication. To use the `entire search` command for accessing checkpoint history and transcripts, you'll need to authenticate by running:

```
entire login
```

This will allow me to search through the repository's checkpoint history and transcripts for i18n-related work. Without authentication, I cannot access the historical search functionality needed to thoroughly answer your question about i18n work.

Would you like to authenticate with Entire, or would you prefer I search the current state of the codebase for i18n-related files and implementations?

Look for mentions of i18n, internationalization, translations, language support, react-i18next, i18next, or any related setup. Report what was done, what files were changed, and what the current state was. Be thorough.

content.text:
The Entire CLI is installed but requires authentication. To use the `entire search` command for accessing checkpoint history and transcripts, you'll need to authenticate by running:

```
entire login
```

This will allow me to search through the repository's checkpoint history and transcripts for i18n-related work. Without authentication, I cannot access the historical search functionality needed to thoroughly answer your question about i18n work.

Would you like to authenticate with Entire, or would you prefer I search the current state of the codebase for i18n-related files and implementations?
Let me check the git log and codebase directly for i18n traces.

input.command: find . -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.tsx" -o -name "*.json" | xargs grep -l "i18n\|i18next\|useTranslation\|react-i18next" 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | grep -v ".git" | head -30

content.content:
import i18next from 'i18next';

content.content:
commit dac06be7cbf10f8c1690f3d8e2b79e10e83e3d80
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Wed May 6 11:35:41 2026 +0200

    fix(tests): add I18nextProvider to all component tests + align tab + button
    
    All unit test files that render components using useTranslation() now wrap
    renders with an I18nextProvider so i18n strings resolve correctly instead
    of falling back to raw keys. Use RTL wrapper option so rerender() preserves
    the provider. Also lift the tab-bar "+" button by mb-1.5 so it visually
    aligns with the tab pill content baseline.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.text:
Here's where things stand with the i18n work:

**The setup is complete and quite comprehensive.** The last commit (`dac06be7`) was the final piece — wrapping all component tests with `I18nextProvider` so tests resolve i18n strings correctly instead of falling back to raw keys.

**What's in place:**
- `packages/web/src/i18n.ts` — full i18next config with **English + German** translations for all major UI sections: tools, cheatsheet, inspector (all tabs/fields), status bar, command palette, top bar, viewport, and workspace
- Language persisted to `localStorage` under `bim-ai:lang`
- Language switching wired into the command palette (`cmd.language`)

**All 14 components are already migrated** to `useTranslation()`:
- `Viewport`, `ToolPalette`, `AppShell`, `CommentsPanel`, `Inspector`, `InspectorContent`, `LeftRail`, `RedesignedWorkspace`, `StatusBar`, `TabBar`, `TopBar`, `Cheatsheet`, `CheatsheetModal`, `RedesignedCommandPalette`

**All test files** have `I18nextProvider` wrappers.

In short: the i18n work was **done** as of the last commit. There's nothing left to implement — it's fully wired up with EN/DE translations across the entire UI.

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: nice. where can i switch between EN and DE? in the UI or somehwere else?

input.description: Search for language switching logic in UI components

lastPrompt: nice. where can i switch between EN and DE? in the UI or somehwere else?

content.text:
It's only in the **command palette**. Open it with `Cmd+K` (or whatever your keybind is), type "language" or "Switch language", and hit Enter — it toggles between EN and DE and persists the choice to `localStorage`.

There's no UI toggle (flag button, dropdown, etc.) in the top bar or settings panel; it's command-palette-only for now.

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'
